mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 60%+ of periods when FEDFUNDS declines by >0.25pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank tightening, increasing real interest rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns exceed 1.5% in 75%+ of periods when CPIAUCSL remains above 3.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and labor force growth (PAYEMS) signal weakening supply-side capacity, creating inflationary pressures that drive negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 60%+ of periods when INDPRO falls >1.5% and PAYEMS declines >50k.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 4.5% signals labor market tightness that accelerates wage growth, increasing corporate costs and reducing equity earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns exceed 1% in 75%+ of periods when UNRATE remains below 4.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate improving consumer confidence and demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through increased construction sector activity.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in 60%+ of periods when HOUST increases by >5%.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real sales (RSAFS) combined with declining GDP growth (GDPC1) signals weakening aggregate demand, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through reduced corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns exceed 1.5% in 75%+ of periods when RSAFS declines >2% and GDPC1 falls >0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing capacity, improving corporate earnings, and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 60%+ of periods when INDPRO increases by >1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates labor market instability, creating uncertainty that drives negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns exceed 1% in 75%+ of periods when PAYEMS rises >50k and UNRATE increases >0.2pp.
